joined_reference: the hours, a delicately crafted film, is an impressive achievement in spite of a river of sadness that pours into every frame. the best thing that can be said of the picture is that it does have a few cute moments. the movie itself appears to be running on hypertime in reverse as the truly funny bits get further and further apart. boy, has this franchise ever run out of gas. dolman confines himself to shtick and sentimentality -- the one bald and the other sloppy. bursting through the constraints of its source, this is one adapted- from-television movie that actually looks as if it belongs on the big screen. a real movie, about real people, that gives us a rare glimpse into a culture most of us don't know. toes the fine line between cheese and earnestness remarkably well ; everything is delivered with such conviction that it's hard not to be carried away.
